# Project Rules - callme-ai

## Stack
- React + Tailwind CSS only
- No new dependencies without explicit approval

## Compliance (enforced by ESLint + pre-commit hook)
1. No unused imports or variables
2. No console.log statements in committed code
3. Accessibility baseline required: aria labels, semantic HTML, alt text on images
4. No `any` type in TypeScript

## Compliance (enforced by you, Copilot)
1. No inline styles; use Tailwind utility classes
2. No hardcoded user-facing strings; use constants/config
3. Responsive first: mobile-first breakpoints (375px -> 768px -> 1440px)
4. Every route/page needs proper title/meta tags
5. No `!important` anywhere

## Code Style
- Functional components only
- Named exports
- Props destructured in function signature
- Tailwind class order: layout -> spacing -> typography -> color -> state

## Allowed Exceptions (must include short reason)
1. Server logs are allowed only through approved logging utilities
2. Temporary rule overrides must be line-scoped and documented with a comment
3. New dependency exceptions require explicit user approval in the same task

## Definition of Done (for any non-trivial edit)
1. Lint passes for touched files
2. Type checks pass for touched files
3. Changed behavior has test coverage or a written test plan in the PR/task summary
4. Accessibility impact checked for interactive UI changes (keyboard/focus/labels)

## Architecture Boundaries
1. UI layer must not contain provider-specific API routing logic
2. Provider orchestration belongs to service/lib/server layers
3. Shared constants and user-facing text should be centralized

## Error Handling Rules
1. No empty catch blocks without a short reason comment
2. Show user-safe error messages; avoid leaking internal/provider details
3. Add fallback behavior for failed network/provider requests where possible

## On Any Edit Request
1. Follow all rules above
2. Flag violations found in existing touched code
3. Suggest concrete fixes with exact diffs
